article_title: FDA Grants Accelerated Approval to Sonrotoclax for Relapsed Mantle Cell Lymphoma With 52% Response Rate in BGB-11417-201
summary: Duplicate of PR #1312 (already merged and published as src/content/articles/2026-05/18-fda-grants-sonrotoclax-accelerated-approval-as-first-bcl-2-inhibitor-for-relapsedrefractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma.md). Same FDA accelerated-approval event, same drug (sonrotoclax / Beqalzi), same BGB-11417-201 trial, same 52% ORR and 15.8-month median DoR. The third duplicate in the triple-overlap cluster (#1312 = primary; #1318 and #1321 = duplicates). Per v3.9.0 policy: 'A duplicate of, or substantially overlaps with, an already-published article — that is REJECT.'

findings.details: PR #1312 was reviewed earlier in this same batch and merged + published at 2026-05-18 ~14:00 UTC. PR #1318 was reviewed and REJECTED as duplicate at ~15:30 UTC. This submission (#1321) is the third independent agent covering the identical event — FDA accelerated approval of sonrotoclax / Beqalzi for R/R MCL on May 13, 2026, BGB-11417-201 / NCT05471843 supporting trial, same 52% ORR / 15.8-month DoR / 11.9-month follow-up specifics. Published article: src/content/articles/2026-05/18-fda-grants-sonrotoclax-accelerated-approval-as-first-bcl-2-inhibitor-for-relapsedrefractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma.md

content_preview.title: FDA Grants Accelerated Approval to Sonrotoclax for Relapsed Mantle Cell Lymphoma With 52% Response Rate in BGB-11417-201
content_preview.summary: BeOne Medicines' Beqalzi clears FDA as the first and only BCL2 inhibitor approved for relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma after a BTK inhibitor, on a single-arm 103-patient Phase 1/2 trial.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted accelerated approval to sonrotoclax (Beqalzi) on May 13, 2026, clearing BeOne Medicines' next-generation BCL2 inhibitor for adults with relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma after at least two lines of systemic therapy including a Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor.

recommendations: REJECT this PR per v3.9.0 policy: 'It's a duplicate of, or substantially overlaps with, an already-published article' → close without merge. PR #1312 is the published primary.

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Third independent agent in the May 18 batch to write up the May 13 FDA Sonrotoclax accelerated approval. Submission is technically clean (valid signature + hash, both biospace.com + FDA sources allowlisted) but the event is already published from PR #1312. Closing without merge.
editor_notes.originality: Triple-duplicate cluster: PRs #1312, #1318, #1321 all covered the same FDA approval. #1312 was the published primary; #1318 and #1321 are both REJECTED as duplicates today.
concerns: Pure duplicate of PR #1312. No new sources adding context not already in the primary, no new specifics not already in the merged article.

Topic:claim atomic gate failure: three agents independently picked up the same FDA approval with slightly different headline framings, and the slug-hash deduplication did not catch them. Three Sonrotoclax PRs in one batch is a system-level issue worth fixing before the next /write-article batch.
recommendations: Future topic:claim improvement: hash by event identifier (FDA approval + drug name + date) rather than slug; the slug hash failed to deduplicate three agents covering the same FDA Sonrotoclax approval with slightly different headline framings (rare-disease oncology approvals are prone to this — agents will gravitate to the same FDA daily news.)
